metadata.title: SYNGO_BP_4003
metadata.comment:
Rationale: Fig 7A and D: Representative traces of mossy fibre field-EPSPs (fEPSPs) before (trace 1) and after (trace 2) induction of mossy fibre LTP in acute slices from littermate WT, Syt12 S97A KI and Syt12 KO mice. mfLTP was induced by two 25 Hz/5 s stimulus trains separated by 20 s and applied in the presence of the NMDA-receptor antagonist d-APV (50 μm) that was washed out immediately after induction. After each individual mfLTP experiment, the slice was exposed to 1 μm DCG-IV, a group II mGluR agonist, to determine the relative contribution of mossy fibre synaptic inputs to the fEPSP.
Figure 7B and E: Mossy fibre fEPSP amplitudes plotted as a function of time before and after mfLTP induction (arrow). Syt12 S97A KI and Syt12 KO mice show a reduction in EPSP amplitude relative to WT.
Figure C and F: Distributions for the magnitude of LTP observed in each genotype were plotted. Significant reduction (~50%) in the magnitude of mfLTP was seen for both Syt12 S97A KI and Syt12 KO mice.

In order to limit the contribution of the population of action potentials in the results, the paper conducted whole-cell recording experiments to check for the effect of Syt12 mutation. Representative traces are displayed in Figure 8A.
Figure 8 B and C: Mossy fibre NMDA-receptor EPSC amplitudes plotted as a function of time before and after mfLTP induction display a reduction in the Syt12 S97A KI condition. Distribution of mfLTP magnitude demonstrated a ~50% reduction in the magnitude of mfLTP for Syt12 S97A KI condition.
Figure 8D: Consistent with its presynaptic expression, mfLTP in WT mice was associated with a decrease in PPR, whereas mfLTP was not accompanied by a significant change in PPR in Syt12 S97A KI mice.

Since the effects of Syt12 KO and Syt12 S97A KI were similar on the magnitude of mossy fibre LTP, the paper proposes that phosphorylation of Syt12 may be required for the expression of LTP in mossy fibres.. Experimental description: Syt12 S97A KI and Syt12 KO mice were generated using homologous recombination in embryonic stem cells targeting exon 4 of the Syt12 gene.
Syt12 S97A KI = A ~15 kb genomic clone containing exon 4 up to the 3' untranslated region (UTR) was isolated using a 32P-labeled probe for exon 4 of Syt12, which was amplified with primers YJ0601 (GCTGTCACCTAGCGATGGTGCC) and YJ0602 (CACGATCTGT TCGTCTGGCAGC). The targeting vector contained a Ser-97-Ala mutation in exon 4, and exon 4 - 6 were flanked with loxP sites for Cre recombination; neomycin resistance gene flanked by frt sites was introduced at a nonconserved region between exon 5 and 6, and a diphtheria toxin (DT) expressing cassette was inserted at the end of 3' UTR.

Syt12 KO = Flp recombinase transgenic mice were used to remove the neomycin resistance cassette for Syt12 S97A KI, followed by breeding to protamine-Cre transgenic mice to generate constitutive Syt12 KO.

Homologous recombination was achieved by electroporation of the linearized targeting construct in R1 embryonic stem cells and positive clones were isolated by drug selection and screened by Southern blotting and PCR genotyping. Clones with confirmed homologous recombination were then injected into C57BL/6 blastocysts
